main_useful_patterns: partial_progress trajectories exist and should be preserved or improved
uncertainties: Do not construct success_like preference pairs because no high-confidence success_like trajectory exists.
Do not treat partial_progress as success_like; only weak preference pairs are allowed.

forbidden_changes: do not only scale all coefficients
do not add global survival bonus without progress gating
do not treat noisy partial_progress as success_like
required_changes: explicitly avoid known failures: early_failure, low_progress_survival
preserve useful patterns: partial_progress trajectories exist and should be preserved or improved

gate or remove components: early_speed_penalty, flight_stability, hovering_penalty, late_stability, low_progress_survival_gate, progress_reward_high, terminal_penalty, ungated_contact_bonus, ungated_survival_bonus
preserve or strengthen components: early_approach, failure_penalty, leg_bonus, low_alt_stability, pad_approach_stability, progress_delta, step_progress, terminal_bonus, terminal_failure_penalty

rollback_decision.reason: Hard rollback is handled by RollbackManager, not Analyzer.

self_evaluation_lesson: Trajectory memory supports preference-guided mutation, but partial progress must remain distinct from success.

previous_selection_used.reason: Selected valid candidate using weak preference evidence.

observed_outcome.main_failure_remaining: ambiguous trajectories dominate (28/50) and low_progress_survival present, no success-like behavior
observed_outcome.main_success_signal: training return improved to -5.57 (best so far), perfect preference consistency (1.0) on memory, no early_failure in training set

failure_repair_outcome.failure_before: Over-penalization of partial progress trajectories in round2 caused regression and rollback; policy collapsed to early failure in training
failure_repair_outcome.repair_attempt: Local repair mutation re-introduced penalty components (early_speed_penalty, late_stability, flight_stability) with adjusted scaling, preserved early_approach and leg_bonus, removed progress_reward_high, kept terminal_penalty and terminal_bonus
failure_repair_outcome.outcome_after: Training return improved to -5.57 (best so far), no early_failure in training set, perfect preference consistency on memory, but ambiguous trajectories still majority (28/50) and no success-like behavior

recent_failure_memory_used.lesson: Local repair that restores basic penalty components with careful scaling can yield significant improvement and perfect memory preference, but ambiguous trajectories remain the bottleneck; future mutations must target ambiguity reduction to convert partial progress into success-like behavior.
future_guidance: Preserve current component structure and scaling that achieved high preference consistency and training return
Investigate ambiguous trajectories to understand distinguishing features from partial progress
Consider adding positive rewards for near-landing states to encourage completion without over-penalization
Avoid reintroducing global alive bonuses or excessive penalties that caused regression in earlier rounds
Monitor training return and preference consistency closely to detect overfitting or divergence

archive_update.reason: Training return -5.57 is best so far, candidate shows significant improvement over previous elites; no rollback triggered.

uncertainties: No success-like trajectories exist, so success-level preference cannot be constructed
Ambiguous trajectories remain poorly understood; their characteristics and how to improve them are uncertain
Exact component contributions to the improvement are not isolated; may be due to combination of scaling and component presence

observed_outcome.main_failure_remaining: No success-like trajectories; early_failure remains (50 out of 125 trajectories) and some components still penalize partial_progress individually
observed_outcome.main_success_signal: Partial progress trajectories are correctly ranked above early_failure overall (preference consistency 1.0) and training return improved to near zero (-0.148)

failure_repair_outcome.failure_before: Partial progress trajectories were over-penalized by early_speed_penalty, late_stability, flight_stability, and terminal_penalty, causing them to be ranked lower than early_failure in some components
failure_repair_outcome.repair_attempt: Local_repair candidate softened or removed these penalties and may have added positive signals for near-landing states
failure_repair_outcome.outcome_after: Overall preference consistency is perfect (1.0) and training return improved, but early_speed_penalty and late_stability components still have zero component consistency, indicating they may still penalize partial_progress relative to early_failure

recent_failure_memory_used.lesson: Softening over-penalizing components can achieve correct overall ordering of partial_progress over early_failure, but individual component misalignment may persist and prevent conversion into success-like behavior; near-landing positive reward is still missing.
future_guidance: Continue to repair early_speed_penalty and late_stability components to have positive component alignment with partial_progress
Add a terminal bonus that is attainable for near-landing states with both legs contact and low velocity
Preserve early_approach and leg_bonus components as they correctly favor higher-quality trajectories
Avoid over-penalizing partial_progress with gated penalties that still disagree
Consider restructuring terminal_penalty to be less severe for partial-progress terminations

observed_outcome.main_success_signal: partial progress increased to 29 trajectories, training return improved to +439.97

failure_repair_outcome.failure_before: zero component consistency for early_speed_penalty and late_stability, missing near-landing positive reward
failure_repair_outcome.repair_attempt: component_recomposition mutation introduced low_alt_stability component, kept early_approach, adjusted terminal components
failure_repair_outcome.outcome_after: preference consistency perfect, failure avoidance high, but low_progress_survival increased (14 vs 1 in round4) and terminal_bonus/penalty still have low alignment

recent_failure_memory_used.lesson: Component recomposition with a well-aligned low_alt_stability component can dramatically improve training return and partial progress count, but the increase in low_progress_survival indicates that near-landing positive reward is still insufficient to convert partial progress into success; future mutations should focus on rewarding successful landing states without causing reward over-inflation.

observed_outcome.main_success_signal: partial_progress exists but reduced to 10 trajectories

failure_repair_outcome.outcome_after: low_progress_survival became dominant (40/50), partial_progress decreased to 10, training return dropped to 6.26, and many components show poor alignment (hovering_penalty 0.058, leg_bonus 0.103, low_alt_stability 0.269, terminal_bonus 0.047)

recent_failure_memory_used.lesson: Component recomposition without careful alignment can cause reward to favor low_progress_survival over partial_progress, leading to regression; future mutations must ensure that new components do not inadvertently reward low-quality trajectories and that the overall preference ordering remains correct.
future_guidance: Preserve early_approach and step_progress components as they show high alignment (0.99 each).
Investigate low_progress_survival trajectories to distinguish features from partial_progress and design components that penalize them.
Avoid or redesign components with low preference consistency: hovering_penalty, leg_bonus (current form), low_alt_stability, terminal_bonus may over-reward low-quality.
Strengthen terminal_bonus or terminal_penalty to favor partial_progress over low_progress_survival.
Consider rollback to round5 elite if further regression occurs.
Continue to avoid global survival bonuses without progress gating.

observed_outcome.main_failure_remaining: low_progress_survival behavior reduced but not eliminated; no success_like trajectories yet
observed_outcome.main_success_signal: failure avoidance improved; low mean reward on failure trajectories (12.95)

failure_repair_outcome.failure_before: low_alt_stability over-rewarded low-altitude survival (low_progress_survival) and terminal_penalty penalized partial_progress more than failures
failure_repair_outcome.repair_attempt: restructured low_alt_stability into pad_approach_stability (gated by pad proximity) and adjusted terminal_penalty to better align with partial_progress
failure_repair_outcome.outcome_after: failure avoidance increased; low_progress_survival trajectories receive low reward, but partial_progress trajectories may not yet be sufficiently preferred over failures due to low leg_bonus and terminal_bonus consistency

recent_failure_memory_used.lesson: Gating low-altitude stability by pad proximity and softening terminal penalty improves failure avoidance, but weak consistency of leg_bonus and terminal_bonus may limit preference for partial_progress over failures; must ensure that partial_progress trajectories receive higher net reward than low_progress_survival to enable conversion to success-like behavior.
future_guidance: monitor leg_bonus and terminal_bonus alignment; consider bounding or restructuring if they over-reward low-quality trajectories
preserve early_approach component which shows strong alignment
ensure net reward on partial_progress is strictly higher than on low_progress_survival to make partial_progress the preferred alternative
avoid adding un-gated survival bonuses
continue to treat partial_progress as distinct from success_like until success_like trajectories appear
